title: Not able to push images to gitlab based registry
body:
I am not anymore able to push an image to a gitlab (11.3.0) based registry:

```
$ podman --version
podman version 0.9.3.1
$ podman push localimage:version gitlab.registry:latest
[...]
DEBU[0019] Checking /v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/sha256:ca63c71cbd98e51b0fae16215e59f39b4f6fca5169c3136da78306217a14d684 
DEBU[0019] HEAD https://registry.gitlab/v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/sha256:ca63c71cbd98e51b0fae16215e59f39b4f6fca5169c3136da78306217a14d684 
DEBU[0019] ... not present                              
Copying blob sha256:ca63c71cbd98e51b0fae16215e59f39b4f6fca5169c3136da78306217a14d684

 8 B / 1.32 GB [>--------------------------------------------------------------]DEBU[0020] PATCH https://registry.gitlab/v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/uploads/5ac73958-3060-4aea-bc34-72970d7e366a?_state=7EMubqw-rJMc8Dxk2yCv8DFLwUclN4SKbS4vZGFi0gF7Ik5hbWUiOiJpbmZyYS9vbW5pYnVzLWdpdGxhYi9naXRsYWIiLCJVVUlEIjoiNWFjNzM5NTgtMzA2MC00YWVhLWJjMzQtNzI5NzBkN2UzNjZhIiwiT2Zmc2V0IjowLCJTdGFydGVkQXQiOiIyMDE4LTEwLTA4VDEzOjUxOjUyLjkzNjE1NTUyWiJ9

 512 B / 1.32 GB [>---------------------------------------------------------] 0s
ERRO[0020] Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Patch https://registry.gitlab/v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/uploads/5ac73958-3060-4aea-bc34-72970d7e366a?_state=7EMubqw-rJMc8Dxk2yCv8DFLwUclN4SKbS4vZGFi0gF7Ik5hbWUiOiJpbmZyYS9vbW5pYnVzLWdpdGxhYi9naXRsYWIiLCJVVUlEIjoiNWFjNzM5NTgtMzA2MC00YWVhLWJjMzQtNzI5NzBkN2UzNjZhIiwiT2Zmc2V0IjowLCJTdGFydGVkQXQiOiIyMDE4LTEwLTA4VDEzOjUxOjUyLjkzNjE1NTUyWiJ9: open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e4fd2f86eeb876027f8fe5d56a867c98cd38bc35d076ded342f13d267e9ce66b/merged/RELEASE: no such file or directory 
```

On the server side the logs look like as followed:

```
Oct 08 15:51:52 gitlab.example.com docker.gitlab[8605]: ==> /var/log/gitlab/registry/current <==
Oct 08 15:51:52 gitlab.example.com docker.gitlab[8605]: 2018-10-08_13:51:51.62086 127.0.0.1 - - [08/Oct/2018:13:51:51 +0000] "PATCH /v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/uploads/bcf72952-e824-4acd-a198-41e82c7f136c?_state=WfU4ouui6zpuuqQ3HzJw1P3kknIrE0KZwJcsu9GhYqZ7Ik5hbWUiOiJpbmZyYS9vbW5pYnVzLWdpdGxhYi9naXRsYWIiLCJVVUlEIjoiYmNmNzI5NTItZTgyNC00YWNkLWExOTgtNDFlODJjN2YxMzZjIiwiT2Zmc2V0IjowLCJTdGFydGVkQXQiOiIyMDE4LTEwLTA4VDEzOjUxOjMzLjg0MjQ3NjI0N1oifQ%3D%3D HTTP/1.1" 202 0 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"

Oct 08 15:51:53 gitlab.example.com docker.gitlab[8605]: ==> /var/log/gitlab/nginx/gitlab_registry_access.log <==
Oct 08 15:51:53 gitlab.example.com docker.gitlab[8605]: 127.0.0.1 - - [08/Oct/2018:13:51:52 +0000] "POST /v2/infra/omnibus-gitlab/gitlab/blobs/uploads/ HTTP/1.1" 202 0 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
```

It looks like gitlab registry answers with an empty response for an uploaded chunk (HTTP Code 202 - size: 0) and libpod is not able to deal with it:

```
DEBU[0020] Error uploading layer chunked, response (*http.Response)(nil) 
```

comments.body:
> It looks like gitlab registry answers with an empty response for an uploaded chunk (HTTP Code 202 - size: 0) and libpod is not able to deal with it:

The “Error uploading layer chunked” is a secondary artifact of the “open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/…/merged/RELEASE: no such file or directory” error; reading the input has failed, and that is reported client-side as the upload failing (because it can’t read the input to send to the server).

The underlying cause is the “…/overlay/…/merged/…: no such file or directory” failure, which has shown up several times in the past few months; I have no idea whether it was tracked down.

I rebuilt the container in question several times and I always get this error. And yes the merged directory is completely empty. So it seems that I am able to reproduce it consistently and the main reason is the empty merge dir.
This sounds like c/storage then. Can you provide more details about the image in question (ideally, a Dockerfile to build it)?

It's basically an alternative Dockerfile to https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/omnibus-gitlab/blob/master/docker/Dockerfile

Content is

```
ROM registry.centos.org/centos/centos:7
MAINTAINER duritong <duritong (~) cronopios.org>

SHELL ["/bin/sh", "-c"]

# Install required packages
RUN yum update -y \
    && yum install --setopt=tsflags=nodocs -y \
      policycoreutils \
      ca-certificates \
      openssh-server \
      openssh-clients \
      hostname \
      patch \
      wget \
      vim \
      tzdata \
    && yum clean all && rm -rf /var/cache/yum \
    && sed 's/session\s*required\s*pam_loginuid.so/session optional pam_loginuid.so/g' -i /etc/pam.d/sshd

# Copy assets
COPY RELEASE /
COPY assets/ /assets/
RUN /assets/setup.el7

# Allow to access embedded tools
ENV PATH /opt/gitlab/embedded/bin:/opt/gitlab/bin:/assets:$PATH

# Resolve error: TERM environment variable not set.
ENV TERM xterm

# Expose web & ssh
EXPOSE 443 80 22

# Define data volumes
VOLUME ["/etc/gitlab", "/var/opt/gitlab", "/var/log/gitlab"]

# Wrapper to handle signal, trigger runit and reconfigure GitLab
CMD ["/assets/wrapper"]

HEALTHCHECK --interval=60s --timeout=30s --retries=5 \
CMD /opt/gitlab/bin/gitlab-healthcheck --fail --max-time 10
```

So I moved my build process to a fully unpriviledge build. Meaning running podman not through sudo and so it's not anymore putting stuff under `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/` and it works fine again. Maybe it's because this path is polluted by a broken overlay?
Yes could you just rm -rf /var/lib/containers and then start over to see if everything works?
I am going to take it that this has been fixed.
